subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of basic practices that depict personal and food hygiene [cite: 2637]
indicator_description: Demonstrate skills of personal hygiene [cite: 2637]
examplars:
1. Think-pair-share the causes of bad body odour. E.g. not bathing well. [cite: 2637]
2. Prepare personal hygiene cards/posters in groups to show causes of bad body odour. [cite: 2637]
3. Identify the appropriate materials used to prevent bad body odour. E.g. Lime/lemon, deodorant. [cite: 2637]
4. Demonstrate how to prevent bad body odour using the materials. [cite: 2637]
5. Plan and organise campaigns to educate the school community on the elimination of bad body odour. [cite: 2637]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of basic practices that depict personal and food hygiene [cite: 2643]
indicator_description: Demonstrate skills in keeping food safe (food hygiene) [cite: 2643]
examplars:
1. Watch videos and pictures of the processes and skills of maintaining food hygiene and write down the observations. E.g. Store food appropriately; Keep hair clean; Cut/trim finger nails short; No wearing of jewelry; Sneeze and cough into a handkerchief; Wash hands regularly. [cite: 2643]
2. Role-play the skills of food hygiene in class. [cite: 2643]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 1.2 PERSONAL, WORKSHOP AND FOOD LABORATORY SAFETY
content_standard: B8.1.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of preventing accidents in the workshop/site/food/sewing laboratory [cite: 2648]
indicator_description: Demonstrate basic skills in applying First Aid to self and others [cite: 2648]
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by First Aid. E.g. It is help given to an injured/sick person till full medical treatment is available. [cite: 2648]
2. List and discuss the contents of a First Aid box. E.g. plaster, gauze, scissors, methylated spirit. [cite: 2648]
3. Demonstrate how to administer first aid to persons affected with any of the following: Cuts, Burns, Suffocation (CPR), Scalds, Falls. [cite: 2648]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.1.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of the basic concept of Environmental health [cite: 2656]
indicator_description: Discuss the causal factors, effects and prevention of desertification and deforestation [cite: 2656]
examplars:
1. Identify the causal factors and discuss the effects and preventive measures of desertification and deforestation, in groups. [cite: 2656]
2. Group Project: Research the causal factors, effects and preventive measures of desertification and deforestation and develop a folder. [cite: 2656]
3. Present project findings in a report for appraisal. [cite: 2656]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.1.3.2 Demonstrate understanding and practice of basic ways of disposing of household and industrial/workshop waste [cite: 2661]
indicator_description: Identify proper management and disposal of household and industrial waste [cite: 2661]
examplars:
1. Research the proper management and disposal of household and industrial waste, in groups. [cite: 2661]
2. Discuss and report on the different ways of disposing off home and industrial waste, proper ways of disposing (landfills, incineration), and ways of recycling. [cite: 2661]
3. Undertake a project on how to recycle waste at home and in school. [cite: 2661]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.2.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of the properties of compliant materials [cite: 2665]
indicator_description: Discuss the basic characteristics of compliant materials [cite: 2665]
examplars:
1. Identify the properties of paper and card board that make them suitable for use. E.g. Paper (medium weight), Cardboard (stiff, smooth). [cite: 2665]
2. Describe the properties of fabrics/textiles that make them suitable for use. E.g. Absorbent, Durable. [cite: 2665]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.2.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of properties of resistant materials [cite: 2673]
indicator_description: Explain the basic properties of resistant materials [cite: 2673]
examplars:
1. Discuss the physical properties of resistant materials. E.g. density, fusibility, electrical/thermal conductivity. [cite: 2673]
2. Investigate the working properties of resistant materials; E.g. strength, hardness, toughness, malleability, ductility, elasticity. [cite: 2673]
3. Make a chart on the various properties of resistant materials. [cite: 2673]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.2.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of properties of resistant materials [cite: 2673]
indicator_description: Describe the properties of building materials [cite: 2673]
examplars:
1. Describe the properties of cement, sand, stones. E.g. cement-binds, Sand-fine, Stone-hard. [cite: 2673]
2. Discuss reasons for choosing a type of material for a building project. [cite: 2673]
3. Prepare a chart on properties of building materials. [cite: 2673]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.2.3.1 Demonstrate understanding and the use of smart and modern materials [cite: 2677]
indicator_description: Discuss smart and modern materials [cite: 2677]
examplars:
1. Identify areas where smart and modern materials are in use. E.g. food, textile, healthcare, building industries. [cite: 2677]
2. Search for products made from smart and modern materials using ICT tools and other sources. E.g. Modified starches, Sanitised fabrics, LCDs, Photochromic pigments. [cite: 2677]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.2.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of the functions of food commodities [cite: 2682]
indicator_description: Explore the functions of food to the body [cite: 2682]
examplars:
1. Classify food according to their basic functions. E.g. body-building, energy-giving and protective foods. [cite: 2682]
2. Relate food commodities to their functions. E.g. Body-building (meat), Energy-giving (cereals), Protective (fruits). [cite: 2682]
3. Draw a chart on the 3 functions of food commodities, and display for appraisal. [cite: 2682]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.3.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of measuring and marking out tools and equipment for production [cite: 2688]
indicator_description: Identify tools and equipment for measuring and marking out [cite: 2688]
examplars:
1. Describe the processes craftsmen/women go through to measure and mark out artefacts in various trade areas (Food lab, Sewing, Building, Wood, Metal). [cite: 2688]
2. Sketch and label parts of measuring and marking out tools and equipment. [cite: 2688]
3. Present the sketched measuring and marking out tools and equipment for appraisal. [cite: 2688]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.3.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of measuring and marking out tools and equipment for production [cite: 2694]
indicator_description: Take measurements of products/artefacts [cite: 2694]
examplars:
1. (A) Take body measurements: Discuss importance and guidelines; Take each other's measurements (bust, waist, hips). [cite: 2694]
2. (B) Take measurements of artefacts/products: Handle tape measure to take measurements in millimeters; Mark out and record measured parts; Indicate dimensions. [cite: 2694]
3. Care for and maintain measuring and marking out tools and equipment. [cite: 2694]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.3.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of measuring and marking out tools and equipment for production [cite: 2698]
indicator_description: Use appropriate techniques to measure [cite: 2698]
examplars:
1. Discuss portion control, equipment used and reasons. [cite: 2698]
2. Plan how to apply portion control in meal preparation and service. [cite: 2698]
3. Discuss the inaccuracies in using measuring tools and equipment that are faulty or tampered with. [cite: 2698]
4. Measure the ingredients for pancake, and make the pancake. [cite: 2698]
5. Measure objects or materials, and make models/mock-ups using compliant and resistant materials. [cite: 2698]
6. Display pancakes and artefacts for appraisal. [cite: 2698]
7. Develop a plan and organise a community education on addressing the issues of using measuring tools that are faulty or tampered with. [cite: 2698]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.3.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of measuring and marking out tools and equipment for production [cite: 2704]
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to care for and maintain measuring and marking out tools used for production [cite: 2704]
examplars:
1. Share experiences from home on how to care for tools and equipment. [cite: 2704]
2. Identify cleaning agents/materials used to clean and maintain tools (Silvo, Brasso, oil). [cite: 2704]
3. Demonstrate how to clean measuring and marking out tools and equipment according to the materials used in making them. [cite: 2704]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.3.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of cutting and shaping tools and equipment for making artefacts/products [cite: 2708]
indicator_description: Identify and use of cutting and shaping tools and equipment [cite: 2708]
examplars:
1. Identify cutting and shaping tools in Building, Wood workshop, and Metal/plastics workshop. [cite: 2708]
2. Discuss the uses of the various types of cutting and shaping tools. [cite: 2708]
3. Sketch and label cutting and shaping tools, and display them for appraisal. [cite: 2708]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.3.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of cutting and shaping tools and equipment for making artefacts/products [cite: 2715]
indicator_description: Use appropriate techniques to design and shape artefacts/products [cite: 2715]
examplars:
1. Use appropriate techniques to shape a design in: Food lab (moulding banku), Sewing (necklines, shorts), Building site, Wood workshop, Metal/plastics workshop. [cite: 2715]
2. Display works for appraisal. [cite: 2715]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.3.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of cutting and shaping tools and equipment for making artefacts/products [cite: 2715]
indicator_description: Use appropriate techniques to cut out marked designs [cite: 2715]
examplars:
1. Use appropriate techniques to cut out a design in: Food lab (biscuit cutting), Sewing, Building site, Wood workshop, Metal/plastics workshop. [cite: 2715]
2. Display products for appraisal. [cite: 2715]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.3.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of cutting and shaping tools and equipment for making artefacts/products [cite: 2715]
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to care for and maintain shaping and cutting tools and equipment [cite: 2715]
examplars: 1. Discuss how to care for and maintain cutting and shaping tools used in Food lab, Sewing, Building, Wood, and Metal workshops. [cite: 2715]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.3.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of joining and assembling materials, tools and equipment used for making artefacts/ products [cite: 2719]
indicator_description: Identify joining and assembling materials, tools and equipment used for making artefacts/products [cite: 2719]
examplars:
1. Identify materials, tools and equipment for joining in Food lab (modern methods), Sewing, Building, Wood, and Metal/plastic workshops. [cite: 2719]
2. Sketch and label tools in each of the trade areas. E.g. Hand sewing machine, clamps [cite: 2719]
3. Display sketches for appraisal. [cite: 2719]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.3.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of joining and assembling materials, tools and equipment used for making artefacts/ products [cite: 2726]
indicator_description: Use appropriate tools, equipment and techniques to join and assemble patterns/artefacts/products [cite: 2726]
examplars:
1. Demonstrate techniques in sewing (handling machine for straight/circular/curved stitching). [cite: 2726]
2. Demonstrate techniques for handling and using the crochet pin/hook in making stitches. [cite: 2726]
3. Demonstrate techniques for joining products made from Wood, Metal, Bricks/blocks, Plastics, and Paper. [cite: 2726]
4. Display specimens and artefacts for appraisal. [cite: 2726]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.3.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of joining and assembling materials, tools and equipment used for making artefacts/ products [cite: 2726]
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to care for and maintain tools and equipment used for joining and assembling [cite: 2726]
examplars: 1. Demonstrate how to care for and maintain tools used in Sewing (dusting/oiling machine), Building, Wood, and Metal/plastic workshops. [cite: 2726]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.3.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of maintaining kitchen essentials [cite: 2732]
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to care for and maintain kitchen essentials [cite: 2732]
examplars:
1. Discuss suitable materials for cleaning kitchen essentials according to the materials used (Aluminum, Stainless steel). [cite: 2732]
2. Demonstrate how to care for and maintain basic kitchen essentials. E.g. washing, cleaning, sterilising. [cite: 2732]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.3.5.1 Demonstrate understanding of application of finishes [cite: 2736]
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to mix the various finishes [cite: 2736]
examplars:
1. Identify tools used for mixing finishes. E.g. containers, stirring rod. [cite: 2736]
2. Demonstrate the procedure for mixing lacquer (diluted 10-15%). [cite: 2736]
3. Demonstrate the procedure for mixing emulsion paint (add water bit by bit). [cite: 2736]
4. Demonstrate the procedure for mixing oil paint (diluted 10-30%). [cite: 2736]
5. Display mixture for appraisal. [cite: 2736]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.3.5.1 Demonstrate understanding of application of finishes [cite: 2742]
indicator_description: Demonstrate skills of finishing edges of sewing articles and food products [cite: 2742]
examplars:
1. Discuss reasons why edges of sewing articles are finished. [cite: 2742]
2. Demonstrate skills of working edge finishes (binding, hemming, pinking). [cite: 2742]
3. Discuss items that can be used as garnishes in the food laboratory. [cite: 2742]
4. Use skills in chopping, dicing, slicing to garnish boiled yam and rice. [cite: 2742]
5. Display specimens/food products for appraisal. [cite: 2742]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: SIMPLE STRUCTURES AND MECHANISMS, ELECTRIC AND ELECTRONIC SYSTEMS
content_standard: B8.4.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of application of principles of forces acting on structures [cite: 2749]
indicator_description: Perform experiments of principles of forces on structures [cite: 2749]
examplars:
1. Research for types of forces (tension, compression, shear, torsion, bending). [cite: 2749]
2. Describe the features of the forces acting on structural members. [cite: 2749]
3. Make sketches and notes of the types of forces. [cite: 2749]
4. Identify suitable resistant materials to perform experiments. [cite: 2749]
5. Perform experiments to show stretching, squashing, sliding, twisting, and bending. [cite: 2749]
6. Write observations and discuss in class. [cite: 2749]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: SIMPLE STRUCTURES AND MECHANISMS, ELECTRIC AND ELECTRONIC SYSTEMS
content_standard: B8.4.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of application of principles of forces acting on structures [cite: 2754]
indicator_description: Design and make simple school technology projects [cite: 2754]
examplars:
1. Identify simple projects (see-saw, mobile stage, bridge). [cite: 2754]
2. Explain reasons for choosing the project. [cite: 2754]
3. Identify suitable materials, tools and equipment. [cite: 2754]
4. Prepare a folio; Plan and make a mock-up; Test and evaluate. [cite: 2754]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.5.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of drawing plane figures and solid objects using drawing instruments [cite: 2759]
indicator_description: Draw plane figures using instruments [cite: 2759]
examplars:
1. Identify 2-D objects (circles, triangles, polygons). [cite: 2759]
2. Draw circles, triangles, quadrilaterals and polygons using instruments. [cite: 2759]
3. Cut shapes of plane figures drawn and prepare an album. [cite: 2759]
4. Use the cut out shapes to make a game (e.g. flash cards). [cite: 2759]
5. Display works for exhibition. [cite: 2759]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.5.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of drawing plane figures and solid objects using drawing instruments [cite: 2759]
indicator_description: Draw objects in pictorial using instruments [cite: 2759]
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by pictorial drawing. [cite: 2759]
2. Identify methods (Isometric, oblique, perspective). [cite: 2759]
3. Illustrate the techniques of drawing objects in these forms. [cite: 2759]
4. Draw objects using instruments and display for appraisal. [cite: 2759]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.5.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of Designing [cite: 2765]
indicator_description: Write a design brief [cite: 2765]
examplars:
1. Observe and write the problem situation. [cite: 2765]
2. State a suitable design brief to address the problem. [cite: 2765]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.5.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of Designing [cite: 2765]
indicator_description: Research into design problems [cite: 2765]
examplars:
1. Develop analysis chart and state questions. [cite: 2765]
2. Conduct research through questionnaires and interview guides. [cite: 2765]
3. Develop observation schedules and take photos/sketches. [cite: 2765]
4. Analyse data and write report. [cite: 2765]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.5.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of Designing [cite: 2765]
indicator_description: Write Design Specifications [cite: 2765]
examplars:
1. Develop and write design specifications based on analysed areas. [cite: 2765]
2. Give reasons for specifications developed. [cite: 2765]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.5.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of Designing [cite: 2770]
indicator_description: Generate Ideas [cite: 2770]
examplars:
1. Use freehand to sketch three possible ideas. [cite: 2770]
2. Write descriptive notes on generated ideas; select the best idea. [cite: 2770]
3. Develop the selected idea and prepare working drawings/folios. [cite: 2770]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.5.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of Designing [cite: 2770]
indicator_description: Make artefact using resistant materials [cite: 2770]
examplars:
1. Study folios, working drawings and cutting lists. [cite: 2770]
2. Identify materials, tools and manufacturing processes. [cite: 2770]
3. Organise environment for health and safety. [cite: 2770]
4. Undertake making in sequence and apply finishing. [cite: 2770]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.5.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of Designing [cite: 2770]
indicator_description: Test and evaluate made products/artefacts [cite: 2770]
examplars:
1. Test using design specifications (shape, function, finish). [cite: 2770]
2. State strengths and weaknesses; propose modifications. [cite: 2770]
3. Rate the quality of the artefact. [cite: 2770]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.5.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of planning for making artefacts/products [cite: 2774]
indicator_description: Plan and make sewing artefacts/products [cite: 2774]
examplars:
1. Explain seams; identify french, open, run and fell, overlaid seams. [cite: 2774]
2. Classify seams (Conspicuous/Inconspicuous); discuss rules and make specimens. [cite: 2774]
3. Identify methods of arranging fullness (Gathering, darts, pleats). [cite: 2774]
4. Make specimens of arrangement of fullness (darts and gathering). [cite: 2774]
5. Display specimens for appraisal. [cite: 2774]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.5.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of planning for making artefacts/products [cite: 2781]
indicator_description: Plan and make wooden, metal and plastic artefacts [cite: 2781]
examplars:
1. Study design folio and critically examine working drawings. [cite: 2781]
2. Study workshop environment for health and safety. [cite: 2781]
3. Identify needs to be addressed (rules, ventilation, tool conditions). [cite: 2781]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.5.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of planning for making artefacts/products [cite: 2781]
indicator_description: Plan and make building artefacts [cite: 2781]
examplars:
1. Describe properties of cement, sand, stones. [cite: 2781]
2. Prepare chart on building material properties. [cite: 2781]
3. Discuss reasons for choice of materials; make mock-ups. [cite: 2781]
4. Display mock-ups for appraisal. [cite: 2781]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 4. MAKING ARTEFACTS FROM COMPLIANT, RESISTANT MATERIALS AND FOOD INGREDIENTS
content_standard: B8.5.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of designing artefacts/ products, and models and planning meals [cite: 2787]
indicator_description: Prepare food using the moist method of cooking [cite: 2787]
examplars:
1. Explain steaming; identify foods (fish, aboloo). [cite: 2787]
2. Discuss types of steaming (pot, plate); advantages and disadvantages. [cite: 2787]
3. Discuss principles/guidelines (tight-fitting lid, circulated steam). [cite: 2787]
4. Prepare a dish using the steaming method and display for appraisal. [cite: 2787]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 4. MAKING ARTEFACTS FROM COMPLIANT, RESISTANT MATERIALS AND FOOD INGREDIENTS
content_standard: B8.5.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of designing artefacts/ products, and models and planning meals [cite: 2792]
indicator_description: Make artefacts/products in crocheting using advanced techniques [cite: 2792]
examplars:
1. Make specimens of advance crocheting stitches (table runners, bags). [cite: 2792]
2. Display specimens for appraisal. [cite: 2792]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 4. MAKING ARTEFACTS FROM COMPLIANT, RESISTANT MATERIALS AND FOOD INGREDIENTS
content_standard: B8.5.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of designing artefacts/ products, and models and planning meals [cite: 2792]
indicator_description: Make artefacts/products using wood, metal and plastics [cite: 2792]
examplars:
1. Organise workshop; check dimensions on cutting list/drawings. [cite: 2792]
2. Follow operation sequence: prepare pieces, measure, mark, cut, assemble. [cite: 2792]
3. Prepare surfaces and apply finish; Test, evaluate and modify. [cite: 2792]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B8.6.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of career opportunities in Career Technology [cite: 2796]
indicator_description: Explore the various career pathways and opportunities in Career Technology [cite: 2796]
examplars:
1. Research the various career pathways and write a brief report. [cite: 2796]
2. Examine your interests, skills and values; compare promised career options. [cite: 2796]
3. Investigate the current demand for the chosen field. [cite: 2796]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 2. ESTABLISHING AND MANAGING A SMALL BUSINESS ENTERPRISE
content_standard: B8.6.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of establishing and managing micro and small business enterprises [cite: 2804]
indicator_description: Explain what is meant by Micro and Small Business Enterprises [cite: 2804]
examplars:
1. Discuss Micro Business Enterprise (capital up to 10,000 cedis, 1-5 workforce). [cite: 2804]
2. Discuss Small Business Enterprise (capital up to 100,000 cedis, 6-29 workforce). [cite: 2804]
3. Identify known businesses and discuss steps to setting them up. [cite: 2804]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 2. ESTABLISHING AND MANAGING A SMALL BUSINESS ENTERPRISE
content_standard: B8.6.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of establishing and managing micro and small business enterprises [cite: 2804]
indicator_description: Explain what is meant by Medium-sized Business Enterprise [cite: 2804]
examplars:
1. Describe Medium-sized Business (capital more than 100,000 cedis, 30+ workforce). [cite: 2804]
2. List four local examples and write down how to set one up. [cite: 2804]
3. Visit nearby enterprises to find out setup procedures and present findings. [cite: 2804]
